verbGlossary.AWAITS CLEARANCE AT: {source}is staged on site but cannot begin work until this {target} is resolved by the responsible party.
verbGlossary.MOBILIZED UPON: {source} or crew is transported to the jobsite and made operational once {target} is reached.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED THROUGH: {source} or load certification through this inspection and {target}.
verbGlossary.OPERATES DURING: {source} is actively running and performing work while the task holds this in-progress {target}.
verbGlossary.IDLES DURING: The equipment sits unused on site, accumulating standby costs, while work is paused at this hold {target}.
verbGlossary.DISPATCHED UPON: {source}is sent to the work area and begins operations once the required {target} is granted.
verbGlossary.LOAD-TESTED VIA: {source}test as part of this inspection and {target} before being put into service.
verbGlossary.TAGGED AT: {source} or scaffold is physically tagged as out-of-service when an inspection finds it at this deficient {target}.
verbGlossary.CLEARED AT: {source} or access platform passes inspection and is marked safe for use at this conforming {target}.
verbGlossary.CONDUCTS: {source} professional performs or leads this inspection and {target} as the responsible party.
verbGlossary.ISSUES FINDING AT: {source}inspector documents a non-conformance or hazard when the inspected item is dispositioned at this deficient {target}.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source}initiates this escalation {target}, alerting supervisors or safety managers.
verbGlossary.INSPECTS AT: {source}inspector examines installed work when it reaches this ready-for-review {target} in the inspection workflow.
verbGlossary.SIGNS OFF AT: {source}inspector formally approves the work by marking it conforming, allowing the next trade to proceed.
verbGlossary.REJECTS AT: {source}inspector fails the installed work at this deficient {target}, requiring the responsible trade to correct and re-present.
verbGlossary.REQUIRES REWORK VIA: {source}inspector's rejection routes the work back through this revise-and-resubmit cycle until it meets specification.
verbGlossary.VERIFIES THROUGH: {source} agent validates system performance by stepping through each stage of this sequential {target}.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONS AT: {source} agent completes final system acceptance and the commissioning record reaches this closed {target}.
verbGlossary.REQUESTS INSPECTION AT: {source} worker notifies QC that their installed work is complete and ready for review at {target}.
verbGlossary.CORRECTS DEFICIENCY VIA: {source} worker fixes rejected work and resubmits it through this revise-and-resubmit cycle for re-inspection.
verbGlossary.PASSES INSPECTION AT: {source}installed work meets specification and is dispositioned at this conforming {target} by the inspector.
verbGlossary.CHARGED UPON: {source}is formally authorized for billing once {target} is reached on the associated change or task.
verbGlossary.PRESSURE-TESTED VIA: {source}fire protection system undergoes hydrostatic pressure testing as part of this inspection and {target}.
verbGlossary.BATCHED UPON: {source} or shop drawing package is submitted for review at {target} before production or delivery begins.
verbGlossary.ACCEPTED WITH CONDITIONS AT: {source}submitted material is approved with noted exceptions or required modifications at this approved-as-noted {target}.
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED FOR APPROVAL AT: The material's shop drawings, mill certs, or product data are formally submitted at {target} for engineer review.
verbGlossary.FABRICATED UPON: {source} is released for off-site fabrication or manufacturing once it achieves this approved {target}.
verbGlossary.RETURNED TO MILL AT: {source} is rejected and sent back to the fabricator or supplier at {target} for redesign or re-sourcing.
verbGlossary.STAKES LAYOUT DURING: {source} establishes control points and marks building lines on site while the layout task is in this active {target}.
verbGlossary.DEMOBILIZED AT: {source} or crew is removed from the project when the associated task has exceeded its deadline at this overdue {target}.
verbGlossary.SCHEDULED BY: {source}is governed by this due date {target} component.
verbGlossary.HELD FOR SUBMITTAL DURING: {source} or installed until its submittal clears this under-review {target} in the approval workflow.
verbGlossary.BACK-ORDERED AT: {source} delivery has missed its required date and the procurement item has escalated to this overdue {target}.
verbGlossary.RELEASED FOR INSTALL UPON: {source} is cleared from the staging area and authorized for field installation once it reaches this approved {target}.
